title: Apprentice - Physical Design & integration
description: Bangalore Area [object Object] Job Description: Job advertising: Apprenticeship in Physical Design & Integration (PDI) Feeling ready to spread your wings ? What if your adventure started with us ?! Whatever your dream job, we might have the Apprenticeship for you ! Are you a fresh graduate looking for an opportunity that will launch your career? Imagine yourself at the heart of an internationally recognized company , established on every continent, and rich in diverse backgrounds, expertise, and cultures. Focused on digital and at the forefront of research and innovation , you have the opportunity to be part of this exciting adventure. Your mission: An apprenticeship opportunity titled : « Apprenticeship - PDI » has just opened at Airbus India located in Bangalore. You will join the "Physical Design and Integration (PDI) " department in charge of System Installation and cabin design activities . Ideally, this is a 12-month program and it should start in [July/2026] (date subject to flexibility). Your main missions will consist of : ● Managing different activities of System installation work packages covering Cabin Design, Mechanical Systems and Electrical Systems and providing support to the others WP/group leaders if needed. ● Network & liaise with a broad range of teams across Airframe, Systems and Cabin sub-domains including Industrialization, Standardization, Research & Environment Integration. Your tutor will support you identify your professional objectives and develop your skills This apprenticeship will allow you to develop the following skills: ● Mechanical System Installation (MSI) ● Electrical System Installation (ESI) ● Cabin Design and DMU Integration ● Configuration Management ● Architecture studies & Automation Your Boarding Pass: Eligibility Criteria: You are a Fresh Graduate (Technical Under Graduate or Post Graduate) in [Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Industrial / Production or Aerospace Engineering] . Zero Work Experience: This program is exclusively for candidates with no prior full-time professional work experience. (Internships and academic projects are excluded from this and are welcome). Commitment: Ability to commit to the full 12-month duration of the apprenticeship. You possess the following competences: - Technical skills : Knowledge on aircraft industrialization principles Knowledge on Aircraft build process Knowledge on Systems Installation and various systems. e.g.Electrical, Mechanical etc. Knowledge in production management & understanding of manufacturing processes Data analytics skills will be an added advantage Basic of scripting in VB, Python will be an added value Exposure to modeling tools is preferred Exposure to Catia V5 tools and DMU knowledge will be an added advantage - Soft Skills : Very good communication and presentation skills Ability to demonstrate effectiveness in holding conversations with customers Ability to work in a fast paced environment with changing priorities or requirements. Demonstrate agility and autonomy in a transnational environment. Demonstrate ability to work as a team in a highly collaborative environment. Ability to coordinate the efforts of a large team of diverse specialists & experts. Ability to lead in an environment of constant change involving multiple internal processes. - Language skills : - English : intermediate / advanced / fluent This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth. description: Bangalore Area [object Object] Job Description: Job advertising template : Apprenticeship Feeling ready to spread your wings ? What if your adventure started with us ?! You will join the “Engineering & PLM” department in charge of building nextgen solutions to help our engineering customers . Ideally, this is a 12-month program and it should start in [ June 2026 ] (date subject to flexibility). Your main missions will consist of : ● Being a software Engineer Assemble and edit source code repositories to develop a software stack for the product needs. Development for fixing defects and new requirements. Migration of applications to new tools as required on projects and initiatives. Respects the standards and structure of the given solution and the environment. Develops design specifications and parameters that are in compliance with products’ architectural blueprints. title: Stage 2026 + Electrical Standard Parts
description: Bangalore Area [object Object] Job Description: Description System standard parts team is a multi-functional team responsible for the development and qualification for both Electrical and Mechanical system standard parts. The group Electrical Standard Parts are responsible for all the electrical and optical system components for aircraft use (Cables, Connectors, Fibre Optics, Circuit-Breakers, Relay, Switches, Electronics components etc.). Each system is connected with an assembly of these parts and our team manages them from specification to in-service support. The parts are used on all Systems and all programmes. Candidates will work directly with procurement, supply chain and manufacturing engineering in a Multi-Functional Team (MFT); and candidates will directly follow many suppliers worldwide. Qualification & Experience: Bachelor Degree in Electrical Engineering or Electrical & Electronics Engineering. Duration : 6 months Required skills and expertise: Basic electrical engineering knowledge Knowledge on Electrical components (eg. Relay, Switches, Circuit Breaker, Electronics components etc) and its working principles. Knowledge of product standards and drawing study Environmental conditions (EMI, corrosion, vibration, banned substance etc.) Aerospace regulatory authority (eg. ATA , EASA, FAA, DGCA etc) Project Management – Basic Proficiency in Python (Data Structures, NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn) for data cleaning and processing, with Google Apps Script has an added advantage. Skillful in crafting and refining prompts to maximize LLM output quality and streamline technical workflows. Soft skills: Very good communication and presentation skills (clear, concise and structured) Ability to work in a fast paced environment with changing priorities, requirements and ramp-up of skills. Demonstrate ability to work as a team in a highly collaborative environment. English – Fluent in English with a good accent (several meetings daily with Engineering, Suppliers, Procurement, Production, ...) Candidates must be able to manage stakeholders through verbal communication. Skills candidate will learn: Electrical Aircraft Architecture. Will gain an overview of many Systems by understanding how they are connected together. New development of system components Qualification, Tests and components for aircraft use Supplier Management. Project Management. This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth. ATA , EASA, FAA, DGCA etc) Project Management – Basic Proficiency in Python (Data Structures, NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn) for data cleaning and processing, with Google Apps Script has an added advantage. Skillful in crafting and refining prompts to maximize LLM output quality and streamline technical workflows. Soft skills: Very good communication and presentation skills (clear, concise and structured) Ability to work in a fast paced environment with changing priorities,
requirements and ramp-up of skills. Demonstrate ability to work as a team in a highly collaborative environment. English – Fluent in English with a good accent (several meetings daily with Engineering, Suppliers, Procurement, Production, ...) Candidates must be able to manage stakeholders through verbal communication. Skills candidate will learn: Electrical Aircraft Architecture. Will gain an overview of many Systems by understanding how they are connected together. New development of system components Qualification, Tests and components for aircraft use Supplier Management. Project Management. Your mission: An apprenticeship opportunity titled : “Industrialisation Engineer” has just opened at Airbus India located in Bengaluru. This position requires a security clearance or requires to be eligible for clearance by recognized authorities. Your main missions will consist of ( either of one below ): You will be responsible for creating 3D Digital Mock-Ups of plants and prepare related methodology documentation and best practices to assist the Industrial teams from conceptual design through the finished industrial system. description: Bangalore Area [object Object] Job Description: Feeling ready to spread your wings ? What if your adventure started with us ?! Whatever your dream job, we might have the Apprenticeship for you ! Are you a fresh graduate looking for an opportunity that will launch your career? Your mission: An apprenticeship opportunity titled : Apprentice - CIO Office (Strategy & Operations) has just opened at Airbus India l ocated in Bangalore. You will join the Digital department of Airbus India. Ideally, this is a 12-month program and it should start in July 2026 (date subject to flexibility). Your main missions will consist of : Office of the CIO Operations: Support the Chief of Staff in managing the daily operations and strategic cadence of the CIO's office, including preparing for executive reviews, tracking key priorities, and managing leadership meeting agendas. Assist in coordinating high-priority, cross-functional projects, ensuring alignment with the CIO's strategic goals. Prepare high-quality presentations, reports, and briefing documents for the CIO and senior leadership. Performance Management: Contribute to the performance management framework for the IT/Digital organization. Assist in tracking Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and Objectives and Key Results (OKRs) against strategic business objectives. Analyze performance data to identify trends, areas for improvement, and potential risks. Dashboard Development & Business Intelligence: Design, develop, and maintain executive-level dashboards (e.g., using Power BI, Tableau, or similar tools) to provide real-time visibility into project status, financial performance, and operational health. Translate complex datasets into clear, actionable insights for senior management. Knowledge Management: Support the development and implementation of a robust Knowledge Management (KM) strategy for the CIO organization. Help establish best practices for capturing, organizing, and disseminating critical information and intellectual property. Identify tools and processes to improve collaboration and information accessibility across digital teams. Your tutor will support you identify your professional objectives and develop your skills This apprenticeship will allow you to develop the following skills: What is in it for you: Direct mentorship from the Chief of Staff and exposure to the CIO and senior leadership team. The opportunity to work on high-impact strategic projects that shape the digital future of Airbus. A front-row seat to C-level decision-making in a leading global technology company. A dynamic, collaborative, and international work environment. Eligibility Criteria: Education: Currently enrolled/just completed in a full-time MBA program from a premier business school. Analytical Skills: Strong quantitative and qualitative analytical skills, with a proven ability to synthesize complex information and make data-driven recommendations. Technical Skills: High proficiency in Google slides (crafting executive-level narratives) and Google sheet (data analysis, modeling). Hands-on experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Looker studio, Power BI, Tableau ) is highly preferred. Familiarity with Knowledge Management tools is a plus. Soft Skills: Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the ability to engage confidently with senior stakeholders. Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. High degree of discretion, professionalism, and integrity in handling confidential information. A self-starter attitude, resourceful, and a creative problem-solver. Professional fluency in English This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth. title: Training admin Intern
description: Gurugram [object Object] Job Description: 1. Training Administration & Scheduling Course Scheduling: Plan and coordinate the complete training calendar, including ground school, simulator sessions (FSTDs - Full Flight Simulators), and aircraft training slots. Resource Management: Schedule and assign qualified instructors (SFIs/TRIs) for training events, ensuring no conflicts and adherence to duty time limitations. Rostering: Manage and publish rosters for trainees and instructors, handling any last-minute changes or requests effectively. Logistics: Arrange all training logistics, including booking classrooms, simulator bays, and necessary training materials. For international trainees, this may also include coordinating travel, accommodation, and visa support. 2. Regulatory Compliance & Documentation Record Keeping: Create, manage, and maintain all trainee records in the company's Training Management System (TMS). This includes logging attendance, training progress, assessment results, and flight hours. Compliance Checks: Ensure all trainees meet the course prerequisites (e.g., valid medical, licenses, previous qualifications) before commencement. Audit Support: Prepare and maintain all documentation for internal quality audits and external audits by the DGCA & EASA Certification: Prepare and issue course completion certificates, type rating qualifications, and other official documentation to trainees upon successful completion of their programs. 3. Stakeholder & Customer Coordination Primary Point of Contact: Serve as the main liaison for trainees, answering inquiries regarding schedules, course requirements, and administrative procedures. Instructor Support: Provide administrative support to the instructional team, ensuring they have the necessary materials, rosters, and trainee information for each session. Internal Liaison: Coordinate closely with other departments, such as FSTD operations, quality assurance, and customer service, to ensure smooth training delivery. Customer Service: Manage relationships with airline customers, providing regular updates on their sponsored trainees and addressing any concerns This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth. description: Bangalore Area [object Object] Job Description: Feeling ready to spread your wings ? What if your adventure started with us ?! Whatever your dream job, we might have the Internship for you ! Are you a fresh graduate looking for an opportunity that will launch your career? Your mission: An internship opportunity titled : « Business Analyst- Quality Methods & Tool » has just opened at Airbus India l ocated in Bangalore . You will join the "Quality" department in charge of Quality for Physical Systems . Ideally, this is a 6 -month program and it should start in [ June/2026 ] (date subject to flexibility). Your main missions will consist of : 1. Testing Strategy, Execution & Quality Assurance (70% Scope) Strategy & Planning : Design and develop comprehensive test strategies, test plans, and detailed test cases specifically for SAP QM functionalities within the product. Execution : Execute various testing cycles, including Functional Testing, Regression Testing, Integration Testing (especially with SAP PP/MM), and User Acceptance Testing (UAT). Defect Management : Manage and track defects throughout the testing life cycle, working closely with developers to ensure timely resolution. Tool Proficiency : Proven ability to create structured test plans and execute testing using tools like SAP Solution Manager (SolMan), Azure DevOps (ADO), or Jira. Compliance : Ensure that all testing activities align with industry standards and internal quality procedures. 2. Functional Analysis (30% Scope) SAP QM Expertise : Leverage deep experience in SAP QM processes, particularly those related to Inspection Processing, Inspection Lot creation, Results Recording, and Usage Decision (UD). Collaborate with various stakeholders to elicit, document, and manage functional and non-functional requirements for new features. Translate high-level business needs into detailed, clear, and unambiguous functional specifications and user stories. Analyze and document current state processes ("As-Is") and model future state processes ("To-Be") using standard tools. 3. Collaboration & Documentation Liaison: Act as the primary liaison between the business unit, SAP configuration/development teams, and external vendor teams. Review Leadership: Lead requirement review meetings, test case reviews, and UAT sessions. Project Artifacts: Maintain and update all project documentation, including requirements traceability matrices (to ensure testing covers all requirements), process flowcharts, and training materials. Your tutor will support you identify your professional objectives and develop your skills This internship will allow you to develop the following skills: Testing Tools: Experience with SAP Solution Manager (SolMan), Azure DevOps (ADO), or Jira SAP Knowledge: Strong, demonstrable experience and knowledge of the SAP Quality Management (QM) module. title: Stagiaire en Développement Cloud, Intern Cloud Developer
description: Quebec, CAN - Remote [object Object] Job Requisition ID # 26WD98608 English will follow Voici une opportunité incroyable pour travailler avec des professionnel.les qui construisent certaines des solutions d'animation 3D les plus avancées pour les effets spéciaux d'Hollywood, les spécialistes de la visualisation, les artistes de jeux et de films. Le groupe Interactive Graphics, Media & Entertainment (IME) est composé d'une gamme de logiciels utilisés par des milliers de créateur.ices et d'artistes de l'industrie des médias et du divertissement, tels que Maya, 3ds Max, Flame et FlowPT. Il s'agit d'une excellente occasion d'acquérir une expérience de développement de première ligne dans un environnement stimulant. Tu travailleras avec une équipe de professionnel.les qui te guideront et partageront leur expertise dans ce domaine passionnant et important du développement de logiciels. Tu pourras acquérir de nouvelles compétences précieuses qui te permettront de faire avancer ta carrière. Veuillez noter qu'il s'agit d'un poste hybride pour l'automne 2026 (période de travail d’août à décembre 2026). Tu seras : Un.e membre actif.ve de notre équipe qui développe des fonctionnalités permettant d'offrir à nos clients la meilleure expérience en temps réel et les meilleurs flux de travail en utilisant les dernières technologies. Inclus.e aux sprints itératifs de conception, de test et de développement pour affiner la solution produit. Tu as/es : Actuellement inscrit.e à un diplôme en informatique, en génie logiciel ou en génie informatique avec une moyenne cumulative d'au moins 3,50 et tu es en deuxième année ou + de ton cheminement. De l'expérience dans le développement d'applications web (ex : React, JS, Redux, Jest, CSS, Node.js, Ruby On Rails, Go, Postgres, Graphql) Un fort intérêt pour les technologies de déploiement en nuage comme Amazon Web Services dans un milieu créatif Un fort souhait de travailler avec les autres, tu communiques bien et tu es prêt à présenter tes idées devant un groupe. Qualifications souhaitées : Technologies web full-stack (React, Ruby On Rails, Java, Python) Expérience des bases de données no-sql (par exemple DynamoDB) Intérêt pour l'infographie et les moteurs de jeu À propos du programme de stages au Canada Le programme 2026 au Canada dure 16 semaines (du 31 août au 18 décembre). Tous les stages sont rémunérés. En tant que stagiaire, vous contribuerez à des projets significatifs, serez encadré par des leaders du secteur, participerez à des conférences techniques et à d'autres activités conçues pour soutenir votre développement personnel et professionnel. Nos stages s'inscrivent dans le cadre de l'approche « Flexible Workplace » d'Autodesk, qui vise à répondre aux besoins de notre entreprise tout en offrant une flexibilité pour soutenir les préférences de travail au bureau, à distance et en mode hybride. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Here is an incredible opportunity to work with professionals who build some of the most advanced 3D animation solutions for Hollywood's special-effects, visualization specialists, game and film artists. The Interactive Graphics, Media & Entertainment (IME) group is composed of a range of software used by thousands of creators and artists in the media and entertainment industry such as Maya, 3ds Max, Flame and FlowPT. This is a great opportunity to get front-line development experience in a stimulating environment. You will work with a team of professionals who will provide guidance and share their expertise in this exciting and important area of software development. Learn some new invaluable skills to push your career to the clouds. Please note that this is a hybrid position for Fall 2026 (August - December 2026 work term). You Will: Be an active member of our team developing features helping provide the best real-time experience and workflows for our customers using the latest in technology Participate in iterative design, testing and development sprints to refine the product solution. You Have: Currently pursuing your degree in Computer Science, Software or Computer Engineering with a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.50 and in your second year and + Experience developing web-based applications (ex: React, JS, Redux, Jest, CSS, Node.js, Ruby On Rails, Go, Postgres, Graphql) Interest in Cloud Deployment Technologies like Amazon Web Services Enjoy working with new technologies in a creative environment You like to work with others, you communicate well and are ok with presenting your ideas in front of a group Preferred Qualifications Full-stack web technologies (React, Ruby On Rails, Java, Python) Exposure to no-sql database (eg DynamoDB) Interest in Computer Graphics / Game Engine About the Canada Intern Program The 2026 Canada program runs for 16 weeks (August 31st – December 18th). description: Quebec, CAN - Remote [object Object] Job Requisition ID # 26WD98603 English will follow Aperçu du poste Voici une opportunité exceptionnelle de collaborer avec des professionnels qui développent certaines des solutions d'animation 3D les plus avancées pour les effets spéciaux hollywoodiens, les spécialistes de la visualisation, les artistes de jeux vidéo et de cinéma. Le groupe Interactive Graphics, Media & Entertainment (IME) propose une gamme de logiciels utilisés par des milliers de créateurs et d'artistes dans l'industrie des médias et du divertissement, tels que Maya, 3ds Max, Flame et ShotGrid. Il s'agit d'une excellente occasion d'acquérir une expérience de développement de premier plan dans un environnement stimulant. Vous travaillerez avec une équipe de professionnels qui vous guideront et partageront leur expertise dans ce domaine passionnant et important du développement logiciel. Vous acquerrez de nouvelles compétences inestimables qui vous permettront de propulser votre carrière vers de nouveaux sommets. Veuillez noter qu'il s'agit d'un poste hybride pour l'automne 2026 (période de travail d’août à décembre 2026). title: Apprentice, Talent Acquisition & People Experience
description: Bengaluru, IND [object Object] Job Requisition ID # 23WD73769 Note: Please add the job role you're looking for from the profile below to the top of your CV/Resume while applying online. Job Title: Apprentice, Talent Acquisition & People Experience Job Location: Bengaluru & Pune Position Overview We are looking for motivated individuals for multiple roles in our People and Places (PPL) division which includes Talent Attraction Coordinator, Early Career Program Coordinator, PPL Advisor, and People Insight Analyst . Talent Attraction Coordinator Role : Responsible for coordinating candidate interviews, manages communication between hiring teams and applicants, and maintains recruitment databases. Collaborate with recruiters to organize recruitment events and contribute to talent acquisition strategies. Ensure a comprehensive onboarding process, handling administrative tasks and maintaining accurate records. Campus Coordinator Role: Responsible for overseeing daily university campus operations, managing recruitment, coordinating with all stakeholders for campus drives. Ensure a smooth campus and candidate experience, organize student engagement programs, and provide support for onboarding activities. PPL Advisor Role : T he PPL Advisor contributes to HR service delivery improvement. Responsibilities include handling HR-related inquiries , case management , and data reporting. Create and interpret reports, manage ServiceNow cases, ensure employee support and m aintain a ccurate employee files. Following Standard Operating Procedures for high-quality and service delivery. People Insight BI (Business Intelligence) Apprentices Role: Data Analyst with exposure in creating visualizations and dashboards for leadership using AWS, Python, R, and SQL. Possess SQL, scripting languages (R, Python), and BI tools (Tableau, Qlikview , Power BI). Collaborate with teams to transform data insights into applicable strategies . People Insight Data Scientist Apprentice Role: Responsible for creating insightful visualizations and dashboards, translating complex data findings into understandable formats for leadership and business. Extract data from diverse sources using AWS, Python/R, SQL, and other automated tools. Perform data analysis to assess quality and obtain meaningful insights. Collaborate with teams, using knowledge in SQL for building complex and optimized queries, scripting languages (R, Python), and BI tools (Tableau, Qlikview , Power BI, etc.) to transform data insights into applicable business strategies . Qualifications: B.tech i n computer science, B .Com , BBA, or a related field (2023 & 2024 graduates only) Thrive in a fast-paced environment with high volume and complex demands Excellent communication and interpersonal skills Ability to work both independently and collaboratively About Autodesk: At Autodesk, we're building a diverse workplace and an inclusive culture to give more people the chance to imagine, design, and make a better world. Autodesk is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and considers all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status or any other legally protected characteristic. We also consider for employment all qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with applicable law. Are you an existing contractor or consultant with Autodesk? Please search for open jobs and apply internally (not on this external site). If you have any questions or require support, contact Autodesk Careers .

title: Internship Counterparty Risk Management (m/f/d) (Ludwigshafen am Rhein, DE)
description: WELCOME TO BASF At Corporate Finance@BASF, we do not just focus on figures, but solutions. Our approach is strategic and innovative, we get things done. Doing so, the needs of our business partners are priority and we have the finger on the pulse when it comes to sustainability and digitalization. Topics such as evaluation of mergers & acquisitions and major investments, financing, the preparation of published statements, the management of financial risks and advising the board of directors on economic decisions are all part of our daily job. In collaboration with our globally networked communities, we continuously develop and enhance our processes. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Our unit, Counterparty Risk Management and Governance, is part of the Corporate Finance department and is responsible for managing counterparty risks with customers and financial institutions. Join our team from September 2026 for a period of 3 to 6 months. Support us in projects related to customer credit risk, trade finance, and bank risk management, and contribute your ideas. Conduct ad-hoc research and analysis based on current macroeconomic developments (e.g., bank mergers, bankruptcies, international conflicts, etc.). Assist us in evaluating short-term country risks and gain exposure to various hedging instruments. Get involved in the analysis of individual major customers. Gain an understanding of the interconnections within a multinational corporation and get introduced to the global customer credit risk community. Bring your experience and knowledge in our digitalization initiatives and actively drive development forward. WHAT YOU OFFER Ongoing studies in economics, business engineering, computer science, or mathematics (no gap year possible) Convincing academic performance and a strong interest in financial issues Basic knowledge of balance sheets, cash flow statements, and profit and loss statements Knowledge of PowerBI, Tableau, SAP, and databases desirable Excellent analytical skills and a consistently accurate and independent work approach Communicative, open-minded personality with convincing English language skills in both written and spoken form WHAT WE OFFER Practical phase - actively apply theory in challenging and varied tasks/projects Teambuddy - contact person for all questions and regular feedback Networks - get to know different perspectives, ways of thinking and fields of work Internship compensation - a recognition of the commitment, more information here: on.basf.com/compensationBASFSE Flexible working hours - depending on the location and area of work HOW TO REACH US Laura Deland (Talent Acquisition), laura.deland@basf.com , Tel.:+49 30 2005-58168 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You're also welcome to book an online appointmen t . You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here ABOUT US BASF SE, located in Ludwigshafen am Rhein, is the corporate headquarters of the BASF Group and part of the Rhine-Neckar Metropolitan Region. BASF stands for competence in chemistry since 1865 and is represented today in the world's major consumer markets, with a wide range of products. As the largest employer in the region, BASF SE offers multiple career opportunities for students, university graduates and professionals providing also training in over 30 professions. Leisure, sports, culture and a good infrastructure characterize the Rhine-Neckar Metropolitan Region, in which BASF is also involved with numerous activities. Learn more about BASF SE at on.basf.com/BASFSE_LU Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us -- in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

title: Internship Predictive Maintenance (m/f/d) (Ludwigshafen am Rhein, DE)
description: WELCOME TO BASF The Global Engineering Services service unit contributes to shaping a sustainable future with outstanding technical expertise. With our dedicated, interdisciplinary teams, we support BASF's divisions in growing together with our customers and markets. We implement investments competitively. Around the globe, we provide safe and reliable solutions to ensure that production plants deliver excellent performance. We are continuously improving and driving digital transformation. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Our Asset Monitoring Solutions & Services unit develops innovative and sustainable solutions to increase plant availability. Unplanned downtimes are specifically reduced by combining process engineering/mechanical engineering knowledge with methods from applied mathematics, data science and IT. The overarching goal is the holistic and sustainable optimization of the maintenance process over the entire life cycle of a plant. As part of a mandatory internship of three to six months, with the option of supplementing this with a voluntary placement, you will have the opportunity to integrate different types of machines into the monitoring process from January 2027 and get to know different IT systems in the process: You will put innovative technologies & methods for model- and data-based condition monitoring of process engineering machines as well as apparatus into practice. You will quickly familiarize yourself with the system architecture and contribute your ideas to the further development of system tools. At the Ludwigshafen site, you will work in an interdisciplinary and cross-departmental team in which you will present and discuss your results. The focus of your work will depend on your interests and skills. WHAT YOU OFFER enrolled student in a bachelor's or master's program in a technical field, e.g. engineering or physics interest in linking plant engineering with digital technologies excellent knowledge of English and good knowledge of German ideally good knowledge of drive and driven machines, process engineering or chemistry motivation and perseverance to implement innovative and practical solutions based on scientific findings independent and autonomous work and ability to actively contribute ideas to the team WHAT WE OFFER Practical phase - actively apply theory in challenging and varied tasks/projects Team buddy - contact person for all questions and regular feedback Networks - get to know different perspectives, ways of thinking and areas of work Internship remuneration - recognition of your work, more information here: http://on.basf.com/VerguetungBASFSE Flexible working hours - depending on the location and area of work HOW TO REACH US Tina Le (Talent Acquisition), tina.le@basf.com, Tel.: +49 30 2005-56367 will be happy to answer your questions about this position. You can also book an online appointment . You can also reach our recruiting team here . You can find initial information on our application process here . The selection process includes a background check of the last diploma obtained and a criminal record certificate. Please submit the required documents only after our request. ABOUT US BASF SE, based in Ludwigshafen am Rhein, is the corporate headquarters of the BASF Group and part of the Rhine-Neckar metropolitan economic area. BASF has stood for competence in chemistry since 1865 and today is represented in the world's major consumer markets with a wide range of products. As the largest employer in the region, BASF SE offers a wide range of career opportunities for students, graduates and professionals and provides training in over 30 professions. Leisure, sport, culture and a good infrastructure characterize the Rhine-Neckar metropolitan region, in which BASF is also involved with numerous activities. You can find out more about BASF SE at: http://on.basf.com/BASFSE_LU Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

title: Intern, Global Controlling & Strategy, Dispersions & Resins (Hong Kong, HK)
description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background. At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees contribute to the BASF Group’s success worldwide. Our business comprises the Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, Surface Technologies, Nutrition & Care and Agricultural Solutions segments. We want to contribute to a better world with enhanced quality of life for everyone. That is why we have firmly anchored the three pillars of sustainability – the economy, environment and society – into our corporate purpose, our strategy, our targets and our operations. They are the core of what we do, a driver for growth and an element of our risk management. We believe that chemistry has a critical role to play in achieving greater sustainability. We are determined to move forward in this respect. Our employees are at the forefront of this transformation, across every role and function in the organization. Join us today in shaping a sustainable future together. About The Role This role is within the Performance Management and Strategic Controlling Team of the Dispersions & Resins Division. Reporting to the team head, the intern will support monthly reports, develop and maintain financial dashboards with Power BI, analyze financial trends, support compliance process and participate in ad hoc projects. How You Will Do It Support the regular management reporting to senior management of our global operating division Develop and maintain financial dashboards using Power BI to transform financial data into valuable insights for business steering Assist in driving digitalization initiatives for reporting and analysis tools Conduct financial trend and variance analysis to identify opportunities for growth and efficiency Assist with ad hoc assignments or projects to support Controlling and Strategic initiatives Participate in global meetings to collaborate with leaders and teams from different regions and functions What We Look For Commitment to a 6-month full-time internship from mid-July 2026 Students of Bachelor’s Degree or Master’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Analytics, or related disciplines Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office tools, including Excel, PowerPoint and Power BI Experience in data analysis or business controlling is a plus Excellent oral and written English communication skills Demonstrates accountability, critical thinking, and strong problem-solving skills A self-starter who can work independently and manage projects with minimal supervision For more information about BASF Internship Program, please visit Your internship at BASF: the foundation for your career BASF has a rich history in Hong Kong, dating back to the 1880s when it began selling textile dyes in the region. Over the years, the company has grown and expanded its presence, establishing its East Asia Regional Headquarters in Hong Kong in 1995. As a multinational company with a long-standing presence in Hong Kong, BASF is committed to the city's continued success as an internationally competitive business and financial center. By the end of 2025, BASF East Asia Regional Headquarters Limited had 240 employees.

title: Trainee Radio & Rigging Engineer (Colchester, GB, CO4 5JF)
description: Recruiter: Trevor Newman Career Grade: TM-2 Internal Closing Date: 12/05/26 Content below for Recruitment purposes only As a Trainee Radio & Rigging engineer in our Networks Field teams, you will enter the business at the ground level. Your traineeship will have you completing a set pathway of internal/external training (dependent on the specific role). You’ll be taught how to work on our network, with a mixture of on the job and classroom training, over a period of 24 months. At the end of this period, you will be assessed, and on successful completion of your assessment will assimilate as a Radio & Rigging Engineer within Networks Field Engineering. About the role: As part of the role you may be required to contribute to out of hours callout by supporting voluntary lists and, if required, Formal On Call Frameworks (with a minimum 1 in 4 attendance) to support service as needed by the business. You'll have the following responsibilities Prioritise H&S – To have regard for health and safety, his/her job license and skills as agreed with line manager. Delivers work with full regard of H&S aspects. Carries out on-site risk assessments giving careful consideration to any hazards before starting work and as the work progresses. Inform manager and record near misses/incidents. Is comfortable challenging and asking others to display their ID passes when on BT premises. Complies with all ISO Audit Requirements, including 14001 and 45001. Inclusive Environment – Responsible for helping to create an inclusive working environment where people from all backgrounds can succeed. Diversity makes sense for us, for our customers and for our future. Effectively lives by the ES values, leading by example at all times. Excellently supports and works with ES teams to deliver, making our unit an amazing workplace for everyone. Radio & Rigging Expertise – Complexity level of work expected: Utilises expertise in technical rigging activities including:. Holds an extensive understanding of a variety of different systems including: Radio – 20CN/21CN/Microwave - installations, systems & maintenance , Mobile – 2/3/4/5g - installations, systems & maintenance ,WIFI systems – installations, systems & maintenance Responsible for achieving & retaining all mandatory training certification including: Tower Climbing and Rescue, Emergency First Aid At Work (At Height) Lifting & lowering equipment: Design & creation of lifting/lowering plans (And associated Risk Assessments), Co-ordinating team member activities during routine/planned works. Supervisory/Clerk of Works responsibility for contractor/3rd party activity within our estate A full breakdown of all skills associated with the role is available in the associated Training Pathways document Must be able to fulfil the physical requirements of the role i.e. able to bend, stretch, kneel and crouch. Also must be able to climb, work at floor and ceiling level inside buildings, tower structures and road side infrastructure, lift heavy drums/cables and recognise coloured wiring. Mandatory: Able to pass a working at height medical. Maintain a level of physical fitness to conduct the role. Openreach is the UK’s leading digital infrastructure business, responsible for building and maintaining the fixed network that connects millions of homes and businesses across the country. While wholly owned by BT Group, Openreach is a legally separate, independently governed business, with its own strategy and operating model. Openreach’s customers are the 600+ communications providers who sell phone, broadband and Ethernet services to UK homes and businesses. Right now, Openreach is building the UK’s largest full fibre broadband network. It’s on track to reach more than 25 million premises by December 2026 and it’s laying the foundations for the country’s connectivity for decades to come. This is nationally significant work, supporting economic growth, regional development and everyday life for millions of people. Operating in a highly regulated environment, Openreach works closely with industry partners, governments and communities to deliver a network that’s reliable, fair and future ready. We have a singular culture that unites all our people: we are customer-first challengers, who are committed, clear and connected. These behaviours unite us as one team to better deliver for our colleagues, our customers, our stakeholders and the country. Joining Openreach means playing a direct role in one of the UK’s most ambitious infrastructure programmes, with real purpose, independence and impact.

title: Stage/Alternance - Assistant(e) Business Performance - (H/F)
description: SEARCH JOBS Stage/Alternance - Assistant(e) Business Performance - (H/F) Location Courbevoie, Île-de-France Region, France Job ID R-246101 Date posted 15/02/2026 APPLY Nous recrutons, dans le cadre d’un lancement de produit multiples indications, un(e) stagiaire ou un(e) alternant(e) en tant qu’Assistant(e) Business Partner Excellence Opérationnelle à partir d'Avril. Contexte : AstraZeneca est un groupe biopharmaceutique international guidé par la science, axé sur la recherche, le développement, la production et la commercialisation de médicaments de prescription. Notre activité se concentre dans des domaines thérapeutiques majeurs : maladies cardiovasculaires et métaboliques, respiratoire, oncologie, neurosciences, maladies inflammatoires et auto-immunes, infectiologie et vaccins. Nous intervenons dans plus de 100 pays et nos médicaments innovants sont utilisés par des millions de patients à travers le monde. Vos missions : En tant qu’Assistant(e) Business Partner Excellence Opérationnelle , vous serez accompagné(e) par le Directeur Business Excellence, vous aurez pour missions principales de : Concevoir et développer des outils de suivi des objectifs des équipes de terrain, Participer à la création de tableaux de bord nécessaires au management des équipes de terrain (unités géographiques, volumes, ratios, critères d’évolutions), Analyser le portefeuille produits de l’entreprise par rapport au marché, Mettre en œuvre, exploiter et présenter des études transversales ad hoc (études de satisfaction, image de l’entreprise...), Participer à la consolidation des études de marché et au contrôle des résultats, Mener une analyse de la performance de l’activité, Mener une analyse comparative de l’organisation des forces de terrain. Votre profil : Vous êtes issu(e) d’une formation Ecole de Commerce ou Ingénieur et, dans l’idéal, avez des connaissances liées à l’environnement de la santé. Vous maîtriser les outils du CRM et du BI ainsi que les méthodologies et concepts SFE. Vous maîtrisez les outils informatiques (Excel, Powerpoint et bases de données). Vous savez faire preuve de rigueur et d’organisation, d’esprit critique et de synthèse. Vous avez le goût du travail en équipe et êtes doté(e) de bonnes aptitudes relationnelles et de communication. Vous êtes curieux(se), volontaire et motivé(e) à l’idée de rejoindre une entreprise et une équipe qui vous confiera de réelles responsabilités. Date Posted 16-févr.-2026 Closing Date AstraZeneca embraces diversity and equality of opportunity. We are committed to building an inclusive and diverse team representing all backgrounds, with as wide a range of perspectives as possible, and harnessing industry-leading skills. We believe that the more inclusive we are, the better our work will be. We welcome and consider applications to join our team from all qualified candidates, regardless of their characteristics. We comply with all applicable laws and regulations on non-discrimination in employment (and recruitment), as well as work authorization and employment eligibility verification requirements. title: Internship - Marketing Intern (1 year)
description: SEARCH JOBS Internship - Marketing Intern (1 year) Location Taipei, Taiwan, Taiwan, China Job ID R-248287 Date posted 17/03/2026 APPLY Please note: This opportunity is available to students who can work onsite at our Taipei office for a full year (20 hours/week). 【Internship Opportunities at AZ Taiwan】 這不只是一份簡單的實習！ 想接觸外商的多元文化與國際接軌？ 想參與跨部門方案與國際專案？ 想了解藥廠人為何感到驕傲特別是在服務病患時？ 這是一個為您量身打造的職場體驗 如果你對醫藥產學有興趣，想要一展長才和抱負，又或是正從校園邁向職場，想要體驗不同的職場生活，千萬不要錯過加入AZ Taiwan的這個機會，我們期待未來與你相見！ 【Main responsibilities】1. Event Execution: Coordinate with internal and external stakeholders to ensure smooth event execution. Assist in the planning and execution of campaigns across various channels. 2. Team Collaboration: Provide administrative support to the marketing team. Assist in creating visual content, such as graphics, images, and videos. Collaborate with agents to produce high-quality marketing materials. 3. Data Collection and Analysis: Conducting research and analysis to support marketing campaigns and projects. Compile and analyze data to support marketing strategies and decision-making. Perform basic statistical analysis and create visualizations. Provide actionable insights and recommendations based on data analysis. 【Who can apply?】 Must be a university student in the third year or above or a graduate student. Working time: at least 2.5 days per week. Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) Experience of graphic design is preferred. Experience of Power BI or any data analytics tool is preferred. Experience of basic understanding of SEO and digital marketing strategies is preferred. Portfolio submission is encouraged. title: Finance & Enterprise Integrations, Associate
description: Finance & Enterprise Integrations, Associate Locations: Bengaluru, India Apply Now Overview Job Description BlackRock Principles Career Path Benefits Overview of Technology roles At BlackRock, technology has always been at the core of what we do – and today, our technologists continue to shape the future of the industry with their innovative work. We are not only curious but also collaborative and eager to embrace experimentation as a means to solve complex challenges. Here you’ll find an environment that promotes working across teams, businesses, regions and specialties – and a firm committed to supporting your growth as a technologist through curated learning opportunities, tech-specific career paths, and access to experts and leaders around the world. Back to nav Job description About this role Team Overview This role is part of Finance and Enterprise Integrations – Platform team. The team owns the end‑to‑end engineering lifecycle for cloud‑native, enterprise‑scale applications, including building, development, testing, deployment, and production support. We work closely with product management, platform engineering, architecture, and operations teams to deliver secure, resilient, and scalable solutions for internal and external clients. Role Description The Associate is an experienced individual contributor responsible for crafting, building, and supporting core backend services within the MyLifePath platform. The position demands strong technical judgment and independence when addressing moderately complex challenges. Associates should develop technical plans, help improve engineering standards, and guide junior engineers. They work closely with product and platform collaborators to provide crucial business functions. Key Responsibilities Engineering & Delivery Build, develop, and maintain backend services and APIs for the MyLifePath platform with clear ownership Implement services using microservices and event‑driven architectures, aligned with enterprise standards Build production‑grade applications using Java (21+) and Kotlin within Spring‑based ecosystems Develop and support integrations with messaging and event platforms (e.g., Azure Service Bus) Translate business and product requirements into robust, scalable technical solutions, including AI‑enabled features AI & Intelligent Platform Skills (Good to Have) Contribute to the delivery of AI‑powered features, including: Retrieval‑Augmented Generation (RAG) patterns for enterprise knowledge and use cases centered on handling documents Integrate backend services with LLM platforms, embedding services, and vector stores in accordance with approved architectures Apply engineering guidelines to AI solutions, including: Leverage approved AI developer assistants (e.g., GitHub Copilot, Windsurf) to improve efficiency while adhering to firm policies Quality, Reliability & Operations Define and implement testing strategies (unit, integration, automated) for both traditional and AI‑enabled components Participate in production support, incident response, and root‑cause analysis Identify technical debt, performance bottlenecks, and reliability risks, driving continuous improvement Apply secure coding practices and resilience patterns to ensure platform stability Collaboration & Influence Contribute to technical build discussions, including AI architecture and integration reviews Partner with product managers, UX, and platform teams to build AI‑enabled user experiences Mentor junior engineers through code reviews and technical mentorship Communicate technical trade‑offs clearly to cross‑functional collaborators Required Technical Skills Core Backend Engineering Strong backend development experience using Java 21+ and/or Kotlin Deep working knowledge of the Spring ecosystem (Spring Boot, Spring Data, Spring Security, Spring Integration) Experience crafting and consuming RESTful APIs and event‑driven services Proficiency with JUnit and automated testing practices Experience with Azure Cosmos DB, MS SQL, or similar data stores Hands‑on experience with Docker and Kubernetes Strong understanding of cloud‑native and distributed systems concepts AI & Intelligent Systems (Preferred) Practical experience or strong familiarity with: LLM integration patterns via APIs and backend services Understanding of agentic AI concepts, including orchestration of tools, workflows, and services Awareness of AI risks, governance, and security considerations in enterprise environments Ability to apply AI capabilities to real business problems, not just experimentation Skills You'll Use Every Day Demonstrates independent execution and ownership of assigned problem spaces Applies sound technical judgment to balance quality, speed, and risk Communicates clearly with engineers and cross-functional collaborators Collaborates effectively within and across teams Shows a continuous improvement approach and invests in learning Demonstrates focus on detail and responsibility for production results Domain experience in financial services, retirement, or insurance platforms is a plus Our benefits To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of benefits including a strong retirement plan, tuition reimbursement, comprehensive healthcare, support for working parents and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about. Our hybrid work model BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock. About BlackRock At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress. This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. title: Analyst, Cash Operations
description: Analyst, Cash Operations Location: Mumbai, Maharashtra Team: Reconciliations Job Requisition #: R264432 Date posted: May. 22, 2026 Apply Job description About this role Would you like to use your know-how to make a difference day in and day out at the world's largest asset manager? Look no further! BlackRock is an equal opportunity employer who value diversity in our company. We are pursuing a Vice President in Cash Operations. Come join our team and experience what it feels like to be part of an organization that makes a difference! Team Overview & Job Purpose: Cash Operations is a group of dedicated professionals who are essential to the investment process by ensuring that all BlackRock cash data is accurate for processes including investment management, analytics, compliance, and regulatory. Cash types include, but are not limited to, equities, fixed income, OTC derivative positions (swaps, swaptions, FXs, and synthetics) and collateral. Our team provides information services and solutions in support of all BlackRock and BlackRock Solutions businesses. The roles and responsibilities will include actively managing risk and exposure for BlackRock by ensuring the investment book of record reconciled with external prime record holders daily. The role requires an understanding of the mechanics of the reconciliation process, exception management, troubleshooting issues, driving innovative enhancements, data analysis, maintaining documentation, assisting management in leading processes, and recommending improvements to ensure efficient and accurate processes. Key responsibilities: Ensure reconciliations are successfully completed and troubleshoot any discrepancies Review cash & asset position exceptions daily including researching differences and resolving them in an accurate and timely manner Ensure accuracy of published cash figures Data analysis enhancing transparency, decision drivers and efficiency Work with internal/external stakeholders to ensure issues are being appropriately reviewed and progressing to completion Develop expertise around team control mechanisms and reporting Identify and coordinate handling of priority customer concerns Actively participate in all team meetings including review of key risk and process indicators Understand and adhere to all regulatory requirements impacting the reconciliation activities Ability to evaluate systems, procedures and make recommendations for improvement Participate in various efficiency and capacity-building initiatives, including automation/ standardization efforts Understand upstream processes impactful to the reconciliation and develop ability to correlate the effect of change Maintain accurate process documents and train team members to ensure complete understanding Accurately record metrics related to responsibilities to enable management to have a good view of team productivity and efficiency Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree – concentration in Accounting, Finance, Business or Economics is preferred Understanding of financial instruments, particularly derivative products, is preferred Strong, effective analytical and problem solving skills Data Analytics skills preferred inclusive of coding (VBA, SQL, PYTHON) and/or understanding of data science methods & implementation Organized approach to ensure follow up on outstanding issues and identify appropriate action to be taken Attention to detail to ensure a high degree of accuracy for all deliverables Excellent communication skills to effectively articulate solutions to internal and external stakeholders Ability to multi-task and balance requests without losing sight of overall objectives and deadlines Exceptional decision-making skills and ability to communicate effectively with senior management Ability to work in a team environment and build strong relationships with colleagues and external contacts Ability to work in a fast paced, rapid changing, deadline driven environment Expertise in Microsoft office products with an emphasis on Excel Our benefits To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of benefits including a strong retirement plan, tuition reimbursement, comprehensive healthcare, support for working parents and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about. Our hybrid work model BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock. Guidance on AI use for candidates At BlackRock, AI has long been part of how we work – enhancing decision-making, improving operations, and helping us deliver better outcomes for clients. We encourage candidates to use AI thoughtfully to learn, prepare, and work more effectively; but during our interview process, we want to focus on getting to know you through your own experiences, thinking, and judgment. To support you, we’ve provided guidance on when and how to use AI during our hiring process so you can approach each step with confidence and showcase your best self. About BlackRock At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress. This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. It’s why we’re dedicated to creating an environment where our colleagues feel welcomed, valued and supported with networks, benefits and development opportunities to help them thrive. To learn more about BlackRock, please visit Careers.BlackRock.com. We also encourage you to get to know us on LinkedIn, Instagram, YouTube, X, and TikTok. BlackRock is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to age, disability, family status, gender identity, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation and other protected attributes at law. title: Analyst, WEA Portfolio Consulting
description: Analyst, WEA Portfolio Consulting Location: Mumbai, Maharashtra Team: Sales & Relationship Mgmt Job Requisition #: R264119 Date posted: May. 19, 2026 Apply Job description About this role The Wealth Enablement and Analytics (WEA) team sits within BlackRock’s US Wealth Advisory business and partners closely with Portfolio Design Services, Advisor Engagement, the Sales Organization, and Product Pillar teams to support key commercial priorities. WEA also plays a critical role in supporting the Portfolio Consulting and Practice Management businesses, delivering consulting engagements, education, analytics, technology tools, and thought leadership. These capabilities are designed to help wealth management firms and financial advisors build better portfolios for end investors, scale their business models efficiently, and enhance client acquisition and retention. Job Purpose: This position sits within a dynamic and fast‑growing team responsible for driving Analysis, Scale, and Reporting capabilities. The role requires an energetic professional to work closely with the US‑based US Wealth Advisory – Wealth Enablement and Analytics (WEA) team, delivering customized risk and analytical reporting solutions for US Financial Advisors. The individual will build strong partnerships with US stakeholders to produce comprehensive portfolio reports featuring in‑depth risk and performance analysis aligned to client objectives. Key Responsibilities: Produce and analyzehighly specialized, customized portfolio risk and performance reports(e.g., Model Scaling, Suite Reports) for financial advisors’ portfolios. Customize BlackRock Model Portfolios in accordance with client‑approved investment guidelines. Analyze and update product performance and industry flow data and prepare relevant market and asset‑class commentary across Equity, Fixed Income, and Alternatives. Ensure all deliverables are completedaccurately and within established timelines, maintaining the highest standards of quality. Support ad‑hoc portfolio, returns, and market data reporting requests as required. Develop, maintain, and enhance end‑to‑end process flow documentation for assigned activities. Contribute toefficiency and capacity‑building initiatives, including automation and standardization efforts. Identify inefficiencies within systems, processes, and procedures, and recommend practical improvements. Effectively manage workload to meet deadlines while collaborating with and supporting team members as needed. Qualifications: Post-Graduate - MBA (Finance) from reputed institute Working knowledge of Power BI, VBA and Python will be an added advantage. CFA / FRM certification will be an added advantage. Skills: 2–4 years of relevant experience in investment management, asset management, or the broader financial services industry. Strong understanding offinancial markets, with exposure across asset classes and geographies. Solid knowledge of portfolio risk and performance concepts, including standard deviation, Sharpe ratio, tracking error, and related metrics. Familiarity withmutual funds, ETFs, and portfolio construction principles. Advanced working knowledge of the MS Office suite, particularly Excel, PowerPoint, and Word. Experience with financial data platforms (e.g., Morningstar or similar) is a strong plus. Working knowledge of Power BI, Tableau, VBA, or Python is an added advantage. Ability to remain flexible and responsive to urgent or time‑sensitive requests. Strong attention to detail withexcellent verbal and written communication skills. Tech‑savvy mindset, with a strong interest in leveraging new tools and techniques to enhance reporting workflows. Prior exposure to sales CRM systems and/or risk reporting tools is desirable. Competencies: Strongwritten and verbal communication skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with global counterparts. Highly organized and detail‑oriented, with a strong focus on time management and prioritization. Demonstrates a strong commitment todata quality and integrity, ensuring accuracy across systems and deliverables. Proven ability tomultitask, manage concurrent priorities, and meet deadlines with minimal supervision in a team environment. Flexible and adaptable, with comfort operating in a fast‑paced, rapidly evolving workflow. Strongcritical thinking and problem‑solving abilities,with an analytical approach to decision‑making. Ability to thrive in a deadline‑driven environment, managing multiple projects simultaneously. Collaborative team player with strong analytical skills and attention to detail. Willingness to support flexible shift timings to meet global business requirements. Our benefits To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of benefits including a strong retirement plan, tuition reimbursement, comprehensive healthcare, support for working parents and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about. Our hybrid work model BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock. title: APAC Finance Controller, Associate
description: APAC Finance Controller, Associate Location: Gurgaon, Haryana Team: Financial Reporting Job Requisition #: R264044 Date posted: May. 19, 2026 Apply Job description About this role Business Unit Overview: The mission of Finance is to be trusted business partners and an industry thought leader supporting optimization of stakeholder value and act as a fiduciary by ensuring high quality financial reporting and rigorous risk oversight and controls. The Financial & Prudential Reporting (FPR) team within APAC Controllers is responsible for corporate accounting, statutory and regulatory reporting, financial analysis, and transaction processing across multiple legal entities in the Asia-Pacific region. The APAC FPR function operates through a hub model with a significant India-based team in Gurgaon. Job Purpose: The Associate, Financial & Prudential Reporting (FPR) Controller will be based in the Gurgaon office and will support FPR activities for APAC legal entities. Key responsibilities include statutory and regulatory reporting, corporate accounting, financial analysis, balance sheet reconciliations, and intercompany processes. The individual will be co-located with the broader India-based FPR hub team, working in a collaborative environment alongside colleagues covering multiple APAC jurisdictions. This role requires strong cross-border collaboration with onshore teams and internal stakeholders across functions. The successful candidate will demonstrate a proactive, self-driven attitude with excellent communication skills and a genuine respect for colleagues across diverse cultures and geographies Key Responsibilities: Support monthly, quarterly, and annual financial close processes for APAC entities, including preparation of journal entries, accruals, and reclassifications in Oracle or equivalent ERP systems. Assist in the preparation of statutory financial statements and regulatory filings in accordance with IFRS or local GAAP, ensuring the integrity of financial data and enabling management to make decisions based on monthly financial reports. Perform balance sheet reconciliations and variance analysis to ensure completeness, accuracy, and integrity of financial data, including intercompany transaction processing, transfer pricing analysis, and reconciliation of intercompany balances. Coordinate with external and internal auditors by preparing audit schedules, providing supporting documentation, responding to audit inquiries in a timely manner and preparing audited financial statements for approval. Collaborate proactively with onshore controllers and cross-functional partners across APAC, as well as India-based FPR colleagues, to ensure seamless information flow, knowledge-sharing, and issue resolution. Identify and implement opportunities for operational excellence — including process automation, workflow optimization, and control enhancements — to reduce risk and increase efficiency. Leverage data analysis skills and AI agent tools (e.g., Microsoft Copilot) to streamline reporting processes, enhance data quality, and deliver insights. Drive the One BlackRock Culture by building trust-based relationships, embracing diversity, equity, and inclusion, and fostering a supportive and respectful team environment. Skills and Qualifications: Chartered Accountant (CA), Certified Public Accountant (CPA), or equivalent professional qualification (or actively pursuing). 3–5 years of post-qualification experience in accounting, financial reporting, or controllership, preferably within asset management, financial services, or a Big 4 audit firm. Solid understanding of IFRS, with familiarity (or willingness to learn) local GAAP frameworks across APAC jurisdictions. Proactive and independent, takes ownership of tasks, anticipates challenges, and drives issues to resolution without waiting for direction. Strong communication skills with a demonstrated ability to engage actively and effectively across teams, functions, and geographies—both verbally and in writing. A genuine respect for others and an inclusive mindset: listens attentively, values diverse perspectives, and treats colleagues with empathy and consideration. Demonstrated interest or experience in data analysis and emerging AI agent tools to improve financial processes and reporting. Exceptional attention to detail with a strong commitment to precision, accuracy, and consistency in financial data. Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet tight deadlines in a fast-paced, globally distributed environment. Our benefits To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of benefits including a strong retirement plan, tuition reimbursement, comprehensive healthcare, support for working parents and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about. Our hybrid work model BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock.
